req.title: Create patient
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-001 mandates that a clinician can create a patient resource with validated demographics, and that creation requests missing mandatory elements are rejected with HTTP 422. The test suite covers: (1) a positive case with all mandatory demographics fields present, validating successful creation; (2) a negative case with a missing mandatory field, validating 422 rejection; (3) a boundary case testing the minimum viable patient record with only required fields at their minimum acceptable lengths. Equivalence Partitioning groups valid vs. invalid payloads. Negative Path testing exercises error conditions. Boundary Value Analysis tests minimum field constraints. Decision Table logic validates mandatory field presence rules.

tests.name: test_create_patient_with_all_mandatory_demographics
preconditions: no patient with given identifier exists
actions: POST to /Patient with payload containing resourceType, identifier, name (given+family), birthDate, gender
expected: response contains Location header with patient ID
response body contains patient resource with all submitted demographics
patient resource is idempotent across re-reads
trace: R-HLT-001: positive case—all mandatory elements present

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_family_name
preconditions: family name is mandatory per FHIR specification
actions: POST to /Patient with payload missing family name in name.family field
include all other mandatory fields (identifier, given name, birthDate, gender)
verify HTTP response status and error message
expected: error response indicates 'family' field is required
trace: R-HLT-001: error pathway 'creation with missing mandatory element shall be rejected'

tests.name: test_create_patient_minimum_viable_demographics_boundary
preconditions: minimum field lengths for string values defined in schema
actions: POST to /Patient with all mandatory fields present
use minimum acceptable lengths for string fields (single character given name, single character family name)
use valid boundary date values (e.g., historical but valid birthDate)
expected: minimal demographics accepted without truncation or rejection
trace: R-HLT-001: boundary condition—minimum viable patient record
BVA: testing lower boundary of field length constraints

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_identifier
preconditions: identifier is mandatory per R-HLT-001 demographics validation
actions: include all other mandatory fields (name, birthDate, gender)

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_mandatory_birth_date
preconditions: birthDate is mandatory per demographics validation rules
expected: error response indicates birthDate is required
